1|//
     2|//  FeedVideo.swift
     3|//  Ivx
     4|//
     5|//  Mirrors the production video-platform feed
     6|//  (GET https://api.ivxholding.com/api/ivx/video-platform/feed) — the same
     7|//  Instagram-grade feed the landing page and Android app consume.
     8|//
     9|
    10|import Foundation
    11|
    12|/// JV deal enrichment attached to a feed video (matched via property_id / project_id).
    13|nonisolated struct FeedVideoDeal: Decodable {
    14|    let id: String
    15|    let title: String?
    16|    let price: Double?
    17|    let minInvestment: Double?
    18|    let expectedRoi: String?
    19|    let dealType: String?
    20|    let url: String?
    21|
    22|    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
    23|        case id
    24|        case title
    25|        case price
    26|        case minInvestment = "min_investment"
    27|        case expectedRoi = "expected_roi"
    28|        case dealType = "deal_type"
    29|        case url
    30|    }
    31|}
    32|
    33|/// A ready-to-play video from the production feed, with engagement counts and deal info.
    34|nonisolated struct FeedVideo: Identifiable, Decodable {
    35|    let id: String
    36|    let projectId: String?
    37|    let videoUrl: String
    38|    let hlsUrl: String?
    39|    let posterUrl: String?
    40|    let previewBlurUrl: String?
    41|    let playbackStatus: String?
    42|    let thumbnailUrl: String?
    43|    let title: String?
    44|    let durationSec: Double?
    45|    let width: Double?
    46|    let height: Double?
    47|    let orientation: String?
    48|    let isPinned: Bool?
    49|    let likeCount: Int?
    50|    let commentCount: Int?
    51|    let shareCount: Int?
    52|    let saveCount: Int?
    53|    let viewCount: Int?
    54|    let propertyId: String?
    55|    /// Type A "deal" (investor deal video) | Type B "reel" (project/construction reel).
    56|    let videoType: String?
    57|    /// Featured Investor Video — interleaved by the backend every 3 deal videos.
    58|    let isFeatured: Bool?
    59|    let deal: FeedVideoDeal?
    60|
    61|    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
    62|        case id
    63|        case projectId = "project_id"
    64|        case videoUrl = "video_url"
    65|        case hlsUrl = "hls_url"
    66|        case posterUrl = "poster_url"
    67|        case previewBlurUrl = "preview_blur_url"
    68|        case playbackStatus = "playback_status"
    69|        case thumbnailUrl = "thumbnail_url"
    70|        case title
    71|        case durationSec = "duration_sec"
    72|        case width
    73|        case height
    74|        case orientation = "orientation"
    75|        case isPinned = "is_pinned"
    76|        case likeCount = "like_count"
    77|        case commentCount = "comment_count"
    78|        case shareCount = "share_count"
    79|        case saveCount = "save_count"
    80|        case viewCount = "view_count"
    81|        case propertyId = "property_id"
    82|        case videoType = "video_type"
    83|        case isFeatured = "is_featured"
    84|        case deal
    85|    }
    86|
    87|    /// Adaptive HLS when the pipeline is ready, otherwise the progressive original.
    88|    var bestPlaybackURL: URL? {
    89|        if let hlsUrl, let url = URL(string: hlsUrl) { return url }
    90|        return URL(string: videoUrl)
    91|    }
    92|
    93|    var posterURL: URL? {
    94|        for candidate in [posterUrl, thumbnailUrl, previewBlurUrl] {
    95|            if let candidate, let url = URL(string: candidate) { return url }
    96|        }
    97|        return nil
    98|    }
    99|
   100|    /// Media aspect ratio from the probed dimensions (16:9 fallback).
   101|    var aspectRatio: Double {
   102|        guard let width, let height, width > 0, height > 0 else { return 16.0 / 9.0 }
   103|        return width / height
   104|    }
   105|}
   106|
   107|nonisolated struct FeedVideoResponse: Decodable {
   108|    let videos: [FeedVideo]
   109|}

(End of file - total 109 lines)

output.content:
1|//
     2|//  ProjectReelsViewModel.swift
     3|//  Ivx
     4|//
     5|//  Loads the Project Reels rail — synced with the 3 live IVX projects.
     6|//  Fetches deal-type videos from the production feed AND the live jv_deals
     7|//  so each reel is paired with its full project record for the detail view.
     8|//
     9|
    10|import Foundation
    11|import Observation
    12|
    13|@Observable
    14|final class ProjectReelsViewModel {
    15|    var videos: [FeedVideo] = []
    16|    var deals: [JVDeal] = []
    17|    /// Combined reels — each entry is either a video-backed reel or a
    18|    /// photo-backed project card (so all 3 projects always appear).
    19|    var reels: [ProjectReel] = []
    20|    var isLoading = false
    21|    var errorMessage: String?
    22|
    23|    func load() async {
    24|        guard !isLoading else { return }
    25|        isLoading = true
    26|        errorMessage = nil
    27|
    28|        async let videoResult = VideoFeedService.fetchFeed(limit: 24)
    29|        async let dealsResult = try await JVDealsService.fetchPublishedDeals()
    30|
    31|        do {
    32|            let fetchedVideos = try await videoResult
    33|            let fetchedDeals = try await dealsResult
    34|            videos = fetchedVideos
    35|            deals = fetchedDeals
    36|            reels = buildReels(videos: fetchedVideos, deals: fetchedDeals)
    37|        } catch {
    38|            // If the video feed fails, still show project reels from deals.
    39|            do {
    40|                let fetchedDeals = try await JVDealsService.fetchPublishedDeals()
    41|                deals = fetchedDeals
    42|                reels = buildReels(videos: [], deals: fetchedDeals)
    43|            } catch let dealError {
    44|                errorMessage = dealError.localizedDescription
    45|                print("[ProjectReels] fetch failed: \(dealError.localizedDescription)")
    46|            }
    47|        }
    48|        isLoading = false
    49|    }
    50|
    51|    /// Build the combined reels list: each live project gets a reel, with
    52|    /// video enrichment when a matching feed video exists.
    53|    private func buildReels(videos: [FeedVideo], deals: [JVDeal]) -> [ProjectReel] {
    54|        var reels: [ProjectReel] = []
    55|
    56|        for deal in deals {
    57|            // Find videos matching this deal by title/project name keywords.
    58|            let dealVideos = videos.filter { video in
    59|                guard let videoTitle = video.title?.lowercased() else { return false }
    60|                let dealName = deal.displayName.lowercased()
    61|                let projectName = (deal.projectName ?? "").lowercased()
    62|                return videoTitle.contains(dealName) ||
    63|                       (projectName.isNotEmpty && videoTitle.contains(projectName))
    64|            }
    65|
    66|            reels.append(ProjectReel(
    67|                id: deal.id,
    68|                deal: deal,
    69|                video: dealVideos.first,
    70|                allVideos: dealVideos
    71|            ))
    72|        }
    73|
    74|        // Also include deal videos not matched to any project (standalone reels).
    75|        let matchedVideoIds = Set(reels.flatMap { $0.allVideos.map(\.id) })
    76|        for video in videos where !matchedVideoIds.contains(video.id) {
    77|            reels.append(ProjectReel(
    78|                id: "video-\(video.id)",
    79|                deal: nil,
    80|                video: video,
    81|                allVideos: [video]
    82|            ))
    83|        }
    84|
    85|        return reels
    86|    }
    87|}
    88|
    89|/// One reel entry — a live project paired with its video (if available).
    90|struct ProjectReel: Identifiable {
    91|    let id: String
    92|    let deal: JVDeal?
    93|    let video: FeedVideo?
    94|    let allVideos: [FeedVideo]
    95|
    96|    var hasVideo: Bool { video != nil }
    97|    var hasDeal: Bool { deal != nil }
    98|}
    99|
   100|private extension String {
   101|    var isNotEmpty: Bool { !isEmpty }
   102|}

(End of file - total 102 lines)

output.content:
1|//
     2|//  ContentView.swift
     3|//  Ivx
     4|//
     5|//  Root tab layout — exact mirror of the Android app's tab bar
     6|//  (expo/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx): all 7 tabs visible in the bottom bar
     7|//  (Home, Invest, Market, Portfolio, Chat, Profile, CRM), gold active
     8|//  tint #FFD700, inactive #777777, black bar with #242424 top border,
     9|//  and Chat as the initial tab (Android initialRouteName="chat").
    10|//
    11|//  A custom bar is required because a native SwiftUI TabView collapses
    12|//  tabs 5–7 into a "More" menu, which would not match Android.
    13|//
    14|
    15|import SwiftUI
    16|
    17|/// The 7 tabs, in the exact order the Android app declares them.
    18|enum IVXTab: Int, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
    19|    case home
    20|    case invest
    21|    case market
    22|    case portfolio
    23|    case chat
    24|    case profile
    25|    case crm
    26|
    27|    var id: Int { rawValue }
    28|
    29|    var title: String {
    30|        switch self {
    31|        case .home: return "Home"
    32|        case .invest: return "Invest"
    33|        case .market: return "Market"
    34|        case .portfolio: return "Portfolio"
    35|        case .chat: return "Chat"
    36|        case .profile: return "Profile"
    37|        case .crm: return "CRM"
    38|        }
    39|    }
    40|
    41|    /// SF Symbol equivalents of the Android lucide icons.
    42|    var icon: String {
    43|        switch self {
    44|        case .home: return "house"
    45|        case .invest: return "chart.line.uptrend.xyaxis"
    46|        case .market: return "chart.bar"
    47|        case .portfolio: return "briefcase"
    48|        case .chat: return "message"
    49|        case .profile: return "person"
    50|        case .crm: return "square.grid.2x2"
    51|        }
    52|    }
    53|}
    54|
    55|struct ContentView: View {
    56|    /// Android's Tabs initialRouteName is "chat" — start there too.
    57|    @State private var selectedTab: IVXTab = .chat
    58|
    59|    private let inactiveColor = Color(ivxHex: 0x777777)
    60|    private let borderColor = Color(ivxHex: 0x242424)
    61|
    62|    var body: some View {
    63|        TabView(selection: $selectedTab) {
    64|            HomeView()
    65|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    66|                .tag(IVXTab.home)
    67|
    68|            InvestView()
    69|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    70|                .tag(IVXTab.invest)
    71|
    72|            MarketView()
    73|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    74|                .tag(IVXTab.market)
    75|
    76|            PortfolioView()
    77|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    78|                .tag(IVXTab.portfolio)
    79|
    80|            ChatTabView()
    81|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    82|                .tag(IVXTab.chat)
    83|
    84|            ProfileTabView()
    85|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    86|                .tag(IVXTab.profile)
    87|
    88|            MembersView()
    89|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    90|                .tag(IVXTab.crm)
    91|        }
    92|        .safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom, spacing: 0) {
    93|            customTabBar
    94|        }
    95|        .preferredColorScheme(.dark)
    96|        .background(Color.ivxBackground)
    97|    }
    98|
    99|    /// Bottom bar mirroring Android: black background, 0.5pt #242424 top
   100|    /// border, 10pt semibold labels, gold active / #777777 inactive.
   101|    private var customTabBar: some View {
   102|        HStack(spacing: 0) {
   103|            ForEach(IVXTab.allCases) { tab in
   104|                Button {
   105|                    selectedTab = tab
   106|                } label: {
   107|                    VStack(spacing: 3) {
   108|                        Image(systemName: tab.icon)
   109|                            .font(.system(size: 20, weight: .medium))
   110|                            .frame(height: 24)
   111|                        Text(tab.title)
   112|                            .font(.system(size: 10, weight: .semibold))
   113|                            .lineLimit(1)
   114|                            .minimumScaleFactor(0.8)
   115|                    }
   116|                    .foregroundStyle(selectedTab == tab ? Color.ivxGold : inactiveColor)
   117|                    .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, minHeight: 48)
   118|                    .contentShape(Rectangle())
   119|                }
   120|                .buttonStyle(.plain)
   121|                .accessibilityLabel(tab.title)
   122|                .accessibilityAddTraits(selectedTab == tab ? [.isSelected] : [])
   123|            }
   124|        }
   125|        .padding(.top, 6)
   126|        .padding(.bottom, 2)
   127|        .background(Color.ivxBackground.ignoresSafeArea(edges: .bottom))
   128|        .overlay(alignment: .top) {
   129|            borderColor.frame(height: 0.5)
   130|        }
   131|    }
   132|}
   133|
   134|#Preview {
   135|    ContentView()
   136|}

(End of file - total 136 lines)

1|//
     2|//  JVDeal.swift
     3|//  Ivx
     4|//
     5|//  Mirrors the Android app's `jv_deals` Supabase rows (expo/lib/jv-storage.ts)
     6|//  so both apps render the exact same live deals — with the full professional
     7|//  detail set (description, partner info, legal terms, fees, photos gallery).
     8|//
     9|
    10|import Foundation
    11|
    12|/// A published JV deal from the production `jv_deals` table.
    13|nonisolated struct JVDeal: Identifiable, Decodable, Hashable {
    14|    let id: String
    15|    let title: String?
    16|    let projectName: String?
    17|    let type: String?
    18|    let status: String?
    19|    let description: String?
    20|    let minInvestment: Double?
    21|    let expectedRoi: Double?
    22|    let totalInvestment: Double?
    23|    let estimatedValue: Double?
    24|    let profitSplit: String?
    25|    let city: String?
    26|    let state: String?
    27|    let country: String?
    28|    let propertyAddress: String?
    29|    let propertyType: String?
    30|    let lotSize: Double?
    31|    let lotSizeUnit: String?
    32|    let zoning: String?
    33|    let termMonths: Int?
    34|    let distributionFrequency: String?
    35|    let exitStrategy: String?
    36|    let startDate: String?
    37|    let endDate: String?
    38|    let governingLaw: String?
    39|    let disputeResolution: String?
    40|    let managementFee: Double?
    41|    let performanceFee: Double?
    42|    let minimumHoldPeriod: Int?
    43|    let partnerName: String?
    44|    let partnerEmail: String?
    45|    let partnerPhone: String?
    46|    let partnerType: String?
    47|    let photos: [String]?
    48|    let published: Bool?
    49|
    50|    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
    51|        case id
    52|        case title
    53|        case projectName = "project_name"
    54|        case type
    55|        case status
    56|        case description
    57|        case minInvestment = "min_investment"
    58|        case expectedRoi = "expected_roi"
    59|        case totalInvestment = "total_investment"
    60|        case estimatedValue = "estimated_value"
    61|        case profitSplit = "profit_split"
    62|        case city
    63|        case state
    64|        case country
    65|        case propertyAddress = "property_address"
    66|        case propertyType = "property_type"
    67|        case lotSize = "lot_size"
    68|        case lotSizeUnit = "lot_size_unit"
    69|        case zoning
    70|        case termMonths = "term_months"
    71|        case distributionFrequency = "distribution_frequency"
    72|        case exitStrategy = "exit_strategy"
    73|        case startDate = "start_date"
    74|        case endDate = "end_date"
    75|        case governingLaw = "governing_law"
    76|        case disputeResolution = "dispute_resolution"
    77|        case managementFee = "management_fee"
    78|        case performanceFee = "performance_fee"
    79|        case minimumHoldPeriod = "minimum_hold_period"
    80|        case partnerName = "partner_name"
    81|        case partnerEmail = "partner_email"
    82|        case partnerPhone = "partner_phone"
    83|        case partnerType = "partner_type"
    84|        case photos
    85|        case published
    86|    }
    87|
    88|    init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
    89|        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
    90|        id = try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .id)
    91|        title = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .title)
    92|        projectName = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .projectName)
    93|        type = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .type)
    94|        status = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .status)
    95|        description = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .description)
    96|        minInvestment = Self.flexibleDouble(container, .minInvestment)
    97|        expectedRoi = Self.flexibleDouble(container, .expectedRoi)
    98|        totalInvestment = Self.flexibleDouble(container, .totalInvestment)
    99|        estimatedValue = Self.flexibleDouble(container, .estimatedValue)
   100|        profitSplit = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .profitSplit)
   101|        city = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .city)
   102|        state = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .state)
   103|        country = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .country)
   104|        propertyAddress = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .propertyAddress)
   105|        propertyType = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .propertyType)
   106|        lotSize = Self.flexibleDouble(container, .lotSize)
   107|        lotSizeUnit = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .lotSizeUnit)
   108|        zoning = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .zoning)
   109|        termMonths = Self.flexibleInt(container, .termMonths)
   110|        distributionFrequency = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .distributionFrequency)
   111|        exitStrategy = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .exitStrategy)
   112|        startDate = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .startDate)
   113|        endDate = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .endDate)
   114|        governingLaw = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .governingLaw)
   115|        disputeResolution = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .disputeResolution)
   116|        managementFee = Self.flexibleDouble(container, .managementFee)
   117|        performanceFee = Self.flexibleDouble(container, .performanceFee)
   118|        minimumHoldPeriod = Self.flexibleInt(container, .minimumHoldPeriod)
   119|        partnerName = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .partnerName)
   120|        partnerEmail = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .partnerEmail)
   121|        partnerPhone = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .partnerPhone)
   122|        partnerType = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .partnerType)
   123|        photos = try? container.decodeIfPresent([String].self, forKey: .photos)
   124|        published = try? container.decodeIfPresent(Bool.self, forKey: .published)
   125|    }
   126|
   127|    /// Convenience for callers that only need the first photo.
   128|    var firstPhoto: String? { photos?.first }
   129|
   130|    private static func flexibleDouble(_ container: KeyedDecodingContainer<CodingKeys>, _ key: CodingKeys) -> Double? {
   131|        if let value = try? container.decodeIfPresent(Double.self, forKey: key) { return value }
   132|        if let text = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: key) { return Double(text) }
   133|        return nil
   134|    }
   135|
   136|    private static func flexibleInt(_ container: KeyedDecodingContainer<CodingKeys>, _ key: CodingKeys) -> Int? {
   137|        if let value = try? container.decodeIfPresent(Int.self, forKey: key) { return value }
   138|        if let text = try? container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: key) { return Int(text) }
   139|        if let double = try? container.decodeIfPresent(Double.self, forKey: key) { return Int(double) }
   140|        return nil
   141|    }
   142|
   143|    /// Display name — Android renders project_name falling back to title.
   144|    var displayName: String {
   145|        let name = (projectName?.isEmpty == false ? projectName : title) ?? "JV Deal"
   146|        return name
   147|    }
   148|
   149|    /// "Southwest Ranches, FL" or the raw property address.
   150|    var displayLocation: String? {
   151|        if let city, !city.isEmpty {
   152|            if let state, !state.isEmpty { return "\(city), \(state)" }
   153|            return city
   154|        }
   155|        return propertyAddress
   156|    }
   157|
   158|    /// Human label for the deal type badge, matching Android copy.
   159|    var typeLabel: String {
   160|        switch (type ?? "").lowercased() {
   161|        case "profit_sharing": return "Profit Sharing"
   162|        case "development": return "Development"
   163|        case "jv": return "JV Partnership"
   164|        case "debt": return "Debt"
   165|        case "equity_split": return "Equity Split"
   166|        case "hybrid": return "Hybrid"
   167|        case "new_construction": return "New Construction"
   168|        case "existing_complete": return "Existing / Complete"
   169|        case "rehab_construction": return "Rehab / Construction"
   170|        default: return (type ?? "JV").capitalized
   171|        }
   172|    }
   173|
   174|    /// Investment option category icons shown on reels and detail.
   175|    /// `tokenized` — fractional/tokenized ownership path.
   176|    /// `jv_deals` — joint venture partnership path.
   177|    /// `buyers` — direct buyer / purchase path.
   178|    var investmentOptions: [InvestmentOption] {
   179|        switch (type ?? "").lowercased() {
   180|        case "jv", "equity_split", "hybrid":
   181|            return [.tokenized, .jvDeals, .buyers]
   182|        case "development", "new_construction", "rehab_construction":
   183|            return [.jvDeals, .tokenized, .buyers]
   184|        case "profit_sharing":
   185|            return [.tokenized, .buyers, .jvDeals]
   186|        default:
   187|            return [.jvDeals, .tokenized, .buyers]
   188|        }
   189|    }
   190|
   191|    /// Formatted term string, e.g. "24 months".
   192|    var termLabel: String? {
   193|        guard let months = termMonths, months > 0 else { return nil }
   194|        if months >= 12 {
   195|            let years = months / 12
   196|            let rem = months % 12
   197|            if rem == 0 { return "\(years) year\(years == 1 ? "" : "s")" }
   198|            return "\(years)y \(rem)m"
   199|        }
   200|        return "\(months) months"
   201|    }
   202|
   203|    /// Full photo gallery — firstPhoto plus any extras.
   204|    var photoGallery: [String] {
   205|        var seen = Set<String>()
   206|        var result: [String] = []
   207|        if let firstPhoto, seen.insert(firstPhoto).inserted { result.append(firstPhoto) }
   208|        if let photos {
   209|            for photo in photos where seen.insert(photo).inserted { result.append(photo) }
   210|        }
   211|        return result
   212|    }
   213|
   214|    // MARK: Hashable — identity-based (used by navigationDestination)
   215|
   216|    func hash(into hasher: inout Hasher) {
   217|        hasher.combine(id)
   218|    }
   219|
   220|    static func == (lhs: JVDeal, rhs: JVDeal) -> Bool {
   221|        lhs.id == rhs.id
   222|    }
   223|}
   224|
   225|/// Investment path icons surfaced on reels and detail screens.
   226|nonisolated enum InvestmentOption: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
   227|    case tokenized
   228|    case jvDeals
   229|    case buyers
   230|
   231|    var id: String { rawValue }
   232|
   233|    var label: String {
   234|        switch self {
   235|        case .tokenized: return "Tokenized"
   236|        case .jvDeals: return "JV Deal"
   237|        case .buyers: return "Buyer"
   238|        }
   239|    }
   240|
   241|    var subtitle: String {
   242|        switch self {
   243|        case .tokenized: return "Fractional ownership"
   244|        case .jvDeals: return "JV partnership"
   245|        case .buyers: return "Direct purchase"
   246|        }
   247|    }
   248|
   249|    var icon: String {
   250|        switch self {
   251|        case .tokenized: return "circle.hexagongrid.fill"
   252|        case .jvDeals: return "person.2.badge.gearshape.fill"
   253|        case .buyers: return "house.and.flag.fill"
   254|        }
   255|    }
   256|
   257|    var tint: ColorProxy {
   258|        switch self {
   259|        case .tokenized: return .gold
   260|        case .jvDeals: return .blue
   261|        case .buyers: return .green
   262|        }
   263|    }
   264|}
   265|
   266|import SwiftUI
   267|
   268|/// Thin color proxy so the enum stays `nonisolated` (no SwiftUI Color dependency).
   269|nonisolated enum ColorProxy {
   270|    case gold, blue, green
   271|
   272|    var color: Color {
   273|        switch self {
   274|        case .gold: return .ivxGold
   275|        case .blue: return .ivxBlue
   276|        case .green: return .ivxGreen
   277|        }
   278|    }
   279|}

(End of file - total 279 lines)

responseMessageContents.text:
Now I understand the full picture. The user wants:
1. Proof the app is live
2. An admin management panel where the owner can add unlimited videos/reels to projects without a developer
3. Currently there are 3 projects with 1 video each - they want the ability to add 2, 3, 4, 5, 6+ videos/reels without limits
4. This should be managed by admin, not requiring a developer

The backend already has:
- POST /api/ivx/video-pipeline/upload — video upload (multipart or sourceUrl)
- POST /api/ivx/video-platform/videos/:videoId/meta — set video_type=reel, property_id, status
- GET /api/ivx/video-platform/feed — the feed
- project_videos table in Supabase

The iOS app has:
- ProjectReelsView — shows reels
- VideoFeedService — fetches from backend
- JVDealsService — fetches deals
- ContentView with 7 tabs

What I need to build:
1. An Admin Reels Manager view in iOS where the owner can:
   - See all projects (3 deals)
   - Add unlimited videos to each project by URL
   - Set video as reel or deal
   - No limit on number of videos
2. Wire it into the Profile tab (admin access)
3. Capture live proof

Let me look at the Profile tab to see where to add the admin entry point.
Let me look at the Profile tab and the live video pipeline to build the admin reels manager.

Let me look at the video pipeline upsertVideoMeta and project_videos insert pattern so I can build an admin "add video by URL" route that registers a video directly into project_videos + platform meta, so the owner can add unlimited reels from the iOS app without a developer.

Let me read the key section of the video pipeline that inserts into project_videos.

Now I understand the full picture. The user wants:

1. Proof that the app is live (backend health, feed, reels)
2. An admin management option in the iOS app where the owner can add more videos to projects/reels WITHOUT a developer — unlimited videos, not just 1 per project. Currently they have 3 projects with 1 video each. They want to add 1,2,3,4,5,6+ videos per project as reels without limits.
3. This should be an admin management feature accessible in the app.

The backend already has:
- `POST /api/ivx/video-pipeline/upload` — accepts multipart file or JSON { sourceUrl }
- `POST /api/ivx/video-platform/videos/:videoId/meta` — set video_type to 'reel', property_id, etc.
- The feed reads from `project_videos` table.

So I need to build:
1. An iOS admin "Manage Reels" view that lets the owner add videos by URL (sourceUrl) to any project, set the video type to 'reel', and see all existing videos per project.
2. This should be accessible from the Profile tab (admin section).
3. No limits on number of videos.

Let me build:
- A new backend endpoint: `POST /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/add-reel` that accepts { sourceUrl, title, projectId, videoType } and calls the pipeline upload + sets meta to reel type.
- Actually, the existing `/api/ivx/video-pipeline/upload` already accepts JSON { sourceUrl, title, projectId }. Then I can call `/api/ivx/video-platform/videos/:videoId/meta` to set video_type to 'reel'. I can do both from the iOS app directly.
- A new iOS `AdminReelsView` that:
  - Lists all videos grouped by project
  - Has an "Add Reel" button that lets the owner paste a video URL, enter a title, select a project, and upload
  - No limit on number of videos
  - Can delete/hide videos too

Let me also add a backend route that combines upload + meta set in one call for simplicity, and returns all videos for management.

Actually, let me create a single backend admin endpoint that:
- `POST /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/reel` — { sourceUrl, title, projectId, videoType: 'reel'|'deal' } → uploads via pipeline + sets meta
- `GET /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/videos` — lists ALL videos with meta info for management
- `POST /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/videos/:videoId/hide` — hide a video
- `POST /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/videos/:videoId/delete` — remove from feed

Actually the existing meta endpoint already handles hide/show. Let me keep it simple and build the iOS admin view that calls the existing endpoints. I'll add one combined admin endpoint for the "add reel by URL" flow.

Let me build this now:

1. Backend: Add `handleAdminAddReel` to ivx-video-platform.ts that calls the pipeline upload + sets meta
2. Backend: Add `handleAdminListVideos` that lists all videos with their meta
3. Wire routes in hono.ts
4. iOS: Create `AdminReelsView.swift` — the management UI
5. iOS: Create `AdminReelsService.swift` — the API service
6. iOS: Add navigation link from ProfileTabView

Let me also capture live proof first.

Let me start building.
Now I have the full picture. Let me build the admin reels manager and capture live proof. I'll create the backend admin endpoints and the iOS admin view in parallel.

responseMessageContents.text:
Done. Here's the summary:

**LIVE PROOF**
- Backend: `https://api.ivxholding.com/health` → **HTTP 200 healthy**, commit `2596b9fe`, 8 feed videos live
- iOS build: **PASSED** (`runChecks` succeeded for `ios-ivx`)
- Proof ledger saved: `backend/verification-proof/ivx-admin-reels-live-proof-2026-07-06.json`

**WHAT WAS BUILT — Admin Reels Manager (no developer needed)**

**Backend (3 new admin endpoints):**
- `GET /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/videos` — list ALL videos (including hidden/draft)
- `POST /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/add-reel` — owner adds a video by pasting a URL
- `POST /api/ivx/video-platform/admin/videos/:videoId` — hide/show, toggle type, feature, delete

**iOS app (2 new files + 1 wired):**
- `AdminReelsService.swift` — API client for all admin video operations
- `AdminReelsView.swift` — full admin UI: stats header (total/reels/deals/hidden), filter chips, video list with thumbnails, add-by-URL sheet, per-video actions (hide/show, set reel/deal, feature, delete)
- `ProfileTabView.swift` — added "Manage Reels & Videos" link under AI & Automation

**HOW IT WORKS (for you, the owner):**
1. Open the app → Profile tab → "Manage Reels & Videos"
2. Tap the **+** button (top right)
3. Paste any video URL (mp4, HLS, etc.), add a title, choose Reel or Deal
4. Tap "Add Reel & Go Live" — the video appears instantly across iOS, Android, and web
5. Add **1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6+ videos — no limit**, no developer needed
6. Each video can be hidden, featured, type-switched, or deleted from the same screen

**NOTE:** The 3 new admin endpoints are in the codebase and the iOS build passed. The live Render deploy is still on commit `2596b9fe` (the previous deploy). To activate the admin endpoints on the live backend, a Render deploy needs to be triggered — want me to push and deploy now?

responseMessageContents.title: Added a new tool for the owner to manage app videos and reels directly.
responseMessageContents.description: This update allows the owner to add unlimited videos to the app just by pasting a link. A new management screen was added to the profile section where you can hide or show videos, categorize them as reels or deals, and feature them on the main feed without needing a developer.
